response: Arbutus and Aspidistra are both types of flowering plants. Arbutus is a genus of 11 accepted species of flowering plants in the family Ericaceae, native to warm temperate regions of the Mediterranean, western Europe, the Canary Islands, and North America. Aspidistra is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asparagaceae, subfamily Nolinoideae, native to eastern and southeastern Asia, particularly China and Vietnam. Both genera are characterized by their ability to grow in shade and their use as foliage plants.
